author: lOrm
content:
Verified tri-state implementation in src/guardrails.rs (commit history references #749 fail-closed refactor that addressed #369 architecturally):

1. GuardrailsState::{Disabled, Enabled(Box<GuardrailsEngine>), Poisoned} enum at L45-53. Holder is LazyLock<Mutex<GuardrailsState>> with no Option wrapper.

2. All six wrapper fns (check_file_access, record_file_modification, check_diff_thresholds, run_quality_gates, reset_turn, get_diff_summary) match all three arms explicitly. Security-boundary check_file_access returns Err(POISON_ERR) on Poisoned (L148-154) — fail-closed, not silent allow. Non-security wrappers log error!() on Poisoned then no-op (loud, not silent). Disabled returns Ok(())/None/empty as the QA-mandated 'no policy loaded' branch.

3. lock_or_poison() helper (L68-80) converts std PoisonError into the Poisoned state instead of swallowing via 'if let Ok(guard) = ...'. grep confirms zero such lock acquisitions remain in the module (only a historical reference inside a test comment at L1946).

Sticky-poison contract enforced: configure() refuses to re-arm a Poisoned state (L118-121).

Regression tests test_749_check_file_access_returns_err_when_poisoned, test_749_check_file_access_happy_path_with_enabled_engine, test_749_configure_refuses_when_poisoned all green. Full guardrails suite: 50 passed. cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features exit 0. No code changes made — pure verify.
